LanguageData.StyleExpand_ApiWarningDesc: Click 'Help' button below for setup guide, or check if API URL is correct
LanguageData.StyleExpand_FileTooLarge: Warning: File '{0}' is large ({1} chars). Processing may take a while.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_ResumeHint: Click 'Resume' to continue from where it stopped
LanguageData.StyleExpand_NotChunked: Style '{0}' is not chunked. Click 'Chunk Style' to process it.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_SelectPawn: Please select a pawn in game first
LanguageData.StyleExpand_NoStyles: No styles found. Place .txt files in the Styles folder.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_NoStyleSelected: No style selected. Select a style above or add .txt files to Styles folder.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_StyleManagementDesc: Place .txt files in the Styles folder, scan and select to chunk
LanguageData.StyleExpand_LlmApiDesc: Use LLM to analyze style characteristics and generate descriptions
LanguageData.StyleExpand_UseRimTalkApiDesc: Reuse RimTalk's LLM API config for generating style descriptions
LanguageData.StyleExpand_LlmRimTalkNotLocal: Please switch RimTalk to Advanced Mode with Local provider (Ollama)
LanguageData.StyleExpand_EmbeddingApiDesc: Convert style text to vectors for chunking and similarity retrieval
LanguageData.StyleExpand_ApiNotConfigured: Please configure Embedding API URL and model name first
LanguageData.StyleExpand_RetrievalConfigDesc: Adjust the number and similarity threshold of retrieved chunks
LanguageData.StyleExpand_SelectChunkedStyle: Please select a chunked style first
LanguageData.StyleExpand_NoChunksRetrieved: No similar chunks found. Try adjusting threshold or different text.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_PromptTemplateDesc: Customize the base style instruction injected to AI
LanguageData.StyleExpand_StylePromptTemplateDesc: LLM prompt for auto-generating style descriptions
LanguageData.StyleExpand_ChunkingConfigDesc: Text chunking settings, controls the length of each chunk
LanguageData.StyleExpand_SemanticChunkingInfo: Semantic chunking: Split by paragraph first, then use semantic similarity for finer splits, ensuring coherent chunks
LanguageData.StyleExpand_RecursiveChunkingInfo: Recursive chunking: Hierarchical splitting by paragraph→sentence→clause, stable and reliable
LanguageData.StyleExpand_EnableSamplingDesc: Automatically sample representative chunks when exceeding threshold
LanguageData.StyleExpand_ShowQueryDesc: Show query in log for each retrieval
LanguageData.StyleExpand_ScribanDesc: Use these variables in RimTalk's advanced mode to inject style prompts:
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpIntro: This MOD makes RimTalk dialogues reflect any writing style you want. Provide style materials (novel excerpts, etc.), and the MOD will analyze and inject style features into conversations.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpStep1: 1. Place style materials (.txt files) in the Styles folder
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpStep3: 3. Select a style, click 'Chunk Style' to process
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpStep4: 4. Wait for chunking to complete (first time requires API calls)
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpStep5: 5. Done! RimTalk dialogues will now reflect this style
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpStep6: 6. Recommended: Configure LLM API and click 'Generate Description' for better results
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpApiEmbedding: Embedding API converts text to vectors, essential for chunking and retrieval. Must be configured to use this MOD.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpApiOllama: Recommended: Use Ollama locally. After installing Ollama, run: ollama pull nomic-embed-text. Set API URL to http://localhost:11434/api/embeddings, model to nomic-embed-text
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpApiOther: Also supports OpenAI, DeepSeek and other compatible APIs. Ensure the model supports embedding.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpStyleOne: - One .txt file = one style. Filename becomes the style name.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpStyleTips: - Materials should contain lots of dialogue and descriptions, with consistent style throughout.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpChunkStrategy: Chunking Strategy: Semantic chunking (recommended) dynamically determines breakpoints based on sentence similarity, ensuring coherent chunks.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpChunkSemantic: Breakpoint Threshold: Higher = longer chunks. 80% is balanced. For dialogue-heavy materials, try 85-90%.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpChunkRecursive: Min/Target/Max Chunk Length: Controls chunk size range. Defaults are optimized for style replication, rarely need adjustment.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpChunkParams: Large File Sampling: Files exceeding threshold will be sampled to representative chunks, avoiding long processing times.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpRetrievalTopK: Top K: How many similar chunks to retrieve per conversation. Default 3 chunks × ~400 chars = ~1200 chars of style material.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpRetrievalThreshold: Similarity Threshold: Chunks below this are filtered out. Default 0.55. Lower to get more material.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpRecommendModels: Recommended Embedding Models: nomic-embed-text (Ollama), mxbai-embed-large (Ollama), text-embedding-3-small (OpenAI)
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpRecommendParams: Recommended Parameters: Semantic chunking, breakpoint 80%, Top K 3, threshold 0.55
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpFaq1: Q: Chunking failed? A: Check API URL and model name. Use 'Test Connection' to verify.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpFaq2: Q: Style effect not obvious? A: Try increasing Top K (5-7), lowering threshold (0.4-0.5), or check if materials have consistent style.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpFaq3: Q: Chunking takes too long? A: Large files take time on first run. Progress is saved, can resume. Keep materials under 20,000 chars.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_HelpFaq4: Q: How to disable style? A: Uncheck 'Enable Style Expand', or deselect in the style list.
LanguageData.StyleExpand_Preview_VariablesHint: Click a variable to insert into query template
LanguageData.StyleExpand_Preview_NoStyleSelected: Please select and chunk a style first
